issue_title: Wrong execution return type with syntax errors and return_promise=True
issue_body:
In the presence of syntax errors in the graphql-query, graphql-server does not respect the `return_promise` execution setting.

The `return_promise` keyword argument is correctly passed down to [graphql-core](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core)'s [`graphql`-function](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core/blob/master/graphql/graphql.py#L33), such that the `ExecutionResult`s are properly wrapped in a `Promise`. [There was a bug in graphql-core](https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core/issues/112) where error-responses would still be unwrapped `ExecutionResults`, which is already fixed.

However, as the parsing of the query is separate from the actual execution, this does not cover errors due to invalid queries (e.g. because of syntax errors). graphql-server will wrap parsing errors in an `ExecutionResult` anyway though and return these as such, without looking at the `return_promise` keyword. I think it's debatable whether parsing errors should be an ExecutionResult at all (seeing as there has been no execution of anything), but as that's the way it is currently implemented at least those should be wrapped in a Promise if the user requested as much.

issue_title: Travis - MacOS PIP not found - xcode 8.3 and 9.2 images
issue_body:
Apparently this was reported a while ago, but today is the first day I'm experiencing it.  Anyone else running into it?  The workaround would be to change the `.travis/install.sh` script i guess... so I'm surprised nobody else has raised it.

issue_title: [DASHBOARD] Static Variable Display Tab
issue_body:
Migrated from https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/issues/4714

> I do a lot of different runs which contain descriptions as well as different meta parameters. It would be nice to have a tab on TensorBoard so I can tell which experiment I am viewing.
> 
> My thought would be to simply add static variables from the Graph which might include Strings, Ints, Floats, (maybe matrix in table format if possible, like PCA parameters, maybe confusion matrix, etc). These may include a text description, current learning rate, network meta parameters, etc. It would be good to simple present the "current" value of these variables so if there is a search on Discount Rates for RL or variation such as learning rate, those can be viewed. This would be different from simply graphing the Learning rate as that shows the history rather than "current". I think of this as a TensorFlow "Dashboard" that I can setup.
> 
> Something like:
> tf.dashboard_summary(tags, tensor/value, collections=None, name=None)
> 
> eg:
> tf.dashboard_summary("Description:",description_string_tensor_or_python_string)
> tf.dashboard_summary("Learning Rate:",lr_tensor)
> tf.dashboard_summary("DiscountRate:",discountRate_python_variable)
> 
> In this case, a "DASHBOARD" tab on tensorflow would contain the 3 labels above and the tensor/value. If the value is a python variable, then it should be considered constant and will not change over the graph lifecycle. If it is a tensor/variable, then it should be pulled from the graph at each iteration.
> 
> I can help but not sure where to jump in to get this started.

issue_body:
## Safari bug with TensorBoard server
### Path not found (404): web-animations-next-lite.min.js.map and weblas.map.json
Safari browser access error with TensorBoard server which displays blank page, after building the TensorBoard github source on a standard Debian VM. This bug has been described many times before and I've tried various fixes unsuccessfully, so it may be something peculiar with my Safari as there is no problem with Chrome/Firefox.

I simplified the setup so the conditions can be recreated independently. My hope is that someone can confirm whether TensorBoard is accessible on their Safari with the below setup.

### System config:
Google Cloud Platform - Compute Engine - VM Instance
Standard 1 vCPU
Debian GNU/Linux 9 (stretch)
Firewall: Allow HTTP/HTTPS traffic
Network: tcp:6010 allow

### Install Git, Bazel, pip and tensorflow:
```bash
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y git openjdk-8-jdk python-pip
echo "deb [arch=amd64] http://storage.googleapis.com/bazel-apt stable jdk1.8" | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/bazel.list
curl https://bazel.build/bazel-release.pub.gpg | sudo apt-key add -
sudo apt-get update && sudo apt-get install -y bazel
sudo apt-get upgrade -y bazel
pip install tensorflow
```

### Clone TensorBoard and Bazel run server:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorboard.git
cd tensorboard
bazel run tensorboard -- --logdir /home/emnist-2000 --host 0.0.0.0 --port 6010
```

### Server output:
When accessing the server with client-side Safari browser, the following server messages appear:
```
TensorBoard 0.4.0a0 at http://0.0.0.0:6010 (Press CTRL+C to quit)
W1104 15:20:57.933438 Thread-2 application.py:268] path /web-animations-next-lite.min.js.map not found, sending 404
W1104 15:20:58.725778 Thread-3 application.py:268] path /weblas.map.json not found, sending 404
```

### Safari error:
Safari (Version 10.0.3 (12602.4.8)) on macOS Sierra (10.12.3), several soft and hard reloads, empty cache, disable cache, restarted.
```
/web-animations-next-lite.min.js.map - Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (NOT FOUND)
/weblas.map.json - Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (NOT FOUND)
```

issue_title: Device can't rejoin network after hardware reset
issue_body:
In a formed OpenThread network, if one device run into a hardware reset (all the parameters will be reset), the device can not rejoin the network again due to `Frame Counter` mismatch, then it will become a leader and form a new partition, and will go through a partition merging process.

Not sure if it is as design, or a potential issue needs to be fixed? And I failed to find any corresponding description in specification talking about this case (rejoin after hardware reset). Appreciate for any feedbacks, thanks.

issue_title: Build: Compiling openthread should not cause build machine to reboot.
issue_body:
I have a fairly stock Ubuntu 16.04 virtual machine that would reboot randomly whenever I would initiate the the stock make commands to build an OpenThread example platform, i.e. the `make -f examples/Makefile-posix` method.

I've isolated the root cause to the large default values for the number of build jobs (`BuildJobs ?= 99`).  I plan to submit a PR to resolve this.

We probably want the default value to be conservatively low so new developers don't have a bad out-of-box experience, and let Travis dial up the numbers within the Continuous Integration scripts.

issue_title: Generación de `name` para datasets (y otras cosas)
issue_body:
**Contexto**

En CKAN varias entidades generan un `name` que actúa como "alias" y tiene caracter identificador (ese `name` apunta únicamente a una entidad y es reemplazable, generalmente, en su uso con el `identifier`).

Actualmente `pydatajson` está usando una función incorrecta para generar el `name` de un dataset:

http://datos.gob.ar/dataset/unidadesterritoriales

debería ser 

http://datos.gob.ar/dataset/unidades-territoriales

La transformación que se espera del nombre de un dataset reemplaza espacios con "-" guiones medios, no los elimina.

**Propuesta**

Existe ya una función en algún módulo de `pydatajson` llamada `title_to_name()`. Esta es la función que debemos usar *siempre* que este tipo de transformación sea requerida, porque nos asegura homogeneidad a lo largo de todas las partes donde se transformen strings de esta manera para generar `names`. 

Esta función debería estar OK, pero si está generando la eliminación de espacios en lugar de su reemplazo por "-", debería corregirse.

issue_title: Incorrect validation accuracy when size of validation set is not divisible by batch_size
issue_body:
Hi,

Thank you so much for providing us such a useful framework.

I recently find a problem.

In https://github.com/tflearn/tflearn/blob/master/tflearn/helpers/trainer.py#L801, the function evaluating validation loss and accuracy, there might be a problem when size of validation set is not divisble by batch_size.

```{python}
def evaluate_flow(session, ops_to_evaluate, dataflow):
        if not isinstance(ops_to_evaluate, list):
            ops_to_evaluate = [ops_to_evaluate]
        tflearn.is_training(False, session)
        dataflow.reset()
        dataflow.start()
        res = [0. for i in ops_to_evaluate]
        feed_batch = dataflow.next()
        n_batches = len(dataflow.batches)
        while feed_batch:
            r = session.run(ops_to_evaluate, feed_batch)
            for i in range(len(r)):
                res[i] += r[i] / n_batches # BUG here
            feed_batch = dataflow.next()
        return res
```

Could I make a pull request to fix the problem? Thank you!

I'am planning to use a LSTM network to predict crimes in the city of montreal. The dataset contains geo-time informations of eahc crimes and the category associated with the crime : 
X = [Day   Hour   Lat  Long ]  ( shape = 54733 * 4 )

`

    net = tflearn.lstm(net,128, return_seq = True)
    net = tflearn.dropout(net, options["dropout"] )
    net = tflearn.lstm(net,128, return_seq = False)

    net = tflearn.fully_connected(net,32,activation='relu')
    net = tflearn.fully_connected(net,1,activation = 'linear')

issue_title: (draft) Support multi node querying for transaction status
issue_body:
### Problem
Given a permissioned network of `N` nodes, capable to tolerate `f` byzantine nodes, such that `N >= 3*f + 1`, a client needs to obtain a trustworthy answer from the network.

As an example, a client may need to know whether a transaction has been committed to the blockchain. How many nodes should the client query?

lower bound: `f + 1`
upper bound: `2*f + 1`

Assuming that `N >= 3*f + 1`, then as soon as a client obtains `f + 1` identical answers, then this answer should be reliable. The client may need to query up to and including `2*f + 1` nodes in order to obtain `f + 1` identical answers.

issue_title: Consider adding synonym capabilities
issue_body:
It would be nice if you could teach cmd2 about synonyms for commands.

Say for example I create a command called **config** which shows the contents of a configuration file. I would like to make that same functionality available in a command called **settings**. I can make that happen by creating a **do_settings()** method which calls **do_config()**, but I have to remember to handle the help messages. This gets more complicated if you are using either of the argparser decorators because you may have to create two parsers, identical except for the **prog** parameter. The other sub-optimal thing is how the **help** command behaves. It shows both commands, but there is no indication that they are synonyms.

It would be great if you could do something like:
```
cmd2.synonyms = { 'config': ['settings', 'my_other_synonym']}
```
and have cmd2 do all the work: make the synonym command names work, with necessary changes to argparser **prog** parameters, and then display the help in such a way that it's easy to see which commands are synonyms for what.

issue_title: Symbol stripping on OSX fails for release build
issue_body:
Hi all,

at least since commit 0f294e26ace5350ef14dfa5391ac8b1725061891, there's symbol stripping functionality in the file tools/pybind11Tools.cmake:

```
    ...
      if(CMAKE_STRIP)
        if(APPLE)
          add_custom_command(TARGET ${target_name} POST_BUILD
                             COMMAND ${CMAKE_STRIP} -u -r $<TARGET_FILE:${target_name}>)
        else()
          add_custom_command(TARGET ${target_name} POST_BUILD
                             COMMAND ${CMAKE_STRIP} $<TARGET_FILE:${target_name}>)
        endif()
      endif()
    ...
```
For some reason this step fails for me on OSX version 10.12. What *does* work, is if I execute
```
$ strip -x mylib.so
```
This strips off about 3MB (going from 21MB to 18MB). I'm not an expert on symbol stripping, so I'm not sure what to do in this situation. The stripped library with the `-x` options seems to work fine in practice. The problem has something to do with other libraries that I link against. Here's the full error when I apply the `-u -r` option:

```
/Applications/Xcode.app/Contents/Developer/Toolchains/XcodeDefault.xctoolchain/usr/bin/strip: symbols referenced by indirect symbol table entries that can't be stripped in: /Users/rwols/dev/llvm/build/release/lib/Clara.so
_llvm_regcomp
_llvm_regerror
_llvm_regexec
_llvm_regfree
_llvm_strlcpy
```
I'm using pybind11 in a "tools" project sitting inside LLVM/Clang, so it's hard for me to come up with a minimal example.

I think a solution for now would be to change `-u -r` into `-x` in the `if(APPLE)` block, but I don't know the ramifications of that change. The man page of `strip` on OSX also tells me:
```
For dynamic shared libraries, the maximum level of stripping is usually -x (to remove all non-global symbols).
```

Finally I'd like to note that the stripping functionality as it is now works fine on Ubuntu both for debug and release builds, and it also works on OSX for debug builds. It fails on OSX for release builds.

issue_title: Follow-ups from enabling HTTP-01
issue_body:
Things we noticed need fixing while we were there (#5409), and code that can be cleaned up.

- `certbot-nginx/certbot_nginx/tls_sni_01.py` and `certbot-nginx/certbot_nginx/http_01.py` could use some deduping -- fix this when we need to
- (nginx) tls sni should have `root` not inside a `location /` block -- probably not worth fixing
- [x] (#5787) (nginx) location is useless as a repeatable directive -- our repeatable directive infrastructure doesn't support blocks as the first argument
- [x] (#5794) code could use deduping internally to `certbot-nginx/certbot_nginx/http_01.py` -- for example, `rewrite_directive` is defined identically twice
- [x] (#5788) Nginx default checking: allow prefix matching of `default_server`, check by port more often, use the first block found listening on that port like Nginx does, be IPv6 sensitive
- [x] (#5793) When copying a default block, remove ipv6only=on because this completely breaks it haha
- "While reviewing, I thought of a few things that are a corner cases of corner cases so it's probably not worth investing much (or maybe any) engineering effort in, but I think in theory we could do better with the address we use on the listen statement in some cases, mainly when creating our own dummy server block.

One case is Certbot takes an --http-01-address flag that matches the port flag. If someone set this, we should ideally respect it and when creating a dummy server block, we should have nginx use that address rather than listening on all interfaces. It's worth noting I didn't do this right in Apache either.

The second case is what if someone sets this and we find an existing server block to use. In theory, I think the behavior would be to use the server block only if it's listening on the same address, but this would likely get complicated when trying to compare the different variations of "listen on all interfaces".

The final case is [now located at #5795].

I think it's probably worth resolving the --http-01-address case for the dummy server block. Not so sure about the other cases." -- add this if anyone ever requests or complains about it

issue_title: HTML Reporter

We should create a third reporter class called `HTMLReporter`, which generates basically the same output as the other reporters, except in HTML form. Note that the output is still just a string printed to the console, but now that string looks like `"

E9999 ...

Some message...

"`, i.e., contains the actual HTML tags. We will use the [Jinja template library](http://jinja.pocoo.org/) to generate the HTML strings. Note that it's likely `jinja` will need to be pip-installed. For now, focus only on generating the raw HTML, and don't worry about styling. It is possible to preview what the HTML looks like raw by pasting the output into a text file, and opening it in a web browser.

issue_title: Forbid on-chain actions which can race with a reorg
issue_body:
These should be unlikely, but to avoid eventual data races we should guard against the cases described below. 

### Problem definition

Given the following execution order, as seen by two Raiden nodes:

On block x, P1 calls settle
On block x+1, P2 calls open channel
On block x+2, P1 calls deposit

With a chain reorg these transactions could be execute in the wrong order, either in the same or different blocks: 

Block x, P2 calls open channel
Block x, P1 calls settle

Or:

Block x, P1 calls deposit
Block x+1, P2 calls open channel

### Tasklist

The only sane strategy to fix this is to send state dependent transaction after the confirmations blocks have elapsed.

- [ ] Register token allowed after the token registry deployment is confirmed
- [ ] Register secret allowed after the token registry deployment is confirmed
- [ ] Open channel allowed after the register token is confirmed
- [ ] Deposit allowed after open is confirmed
- [ ] Withdraw allowed after open is confirmed
- [ ] Close allowed after open is confirmed
- [ ] Settle allowed after the settlement window "is confirmed", IOW after block `close_block + settle_timeout + confirmation_blocks`
- [ ] Unlock allowed after settlement is confirmed

issue_title: Init of created genesis block fails
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

Attempting to create a new network using the tools provided in Raiden results in an invalid genesis json file.  Specifically, there appear to be three issues.  The first is that values for the "code" key should start with "0x" but do not.  For example:

    $ python tools/config_builder.py full_genesis > mycustomgenesis.json
    $ geth --datadir /tmp/privategeth init mycustomgenesis.json
    Fatal: invalid genesis file: missing 0x prefix for hex data
    $

This first issue can be overcome (as a hack) with a sed command, but this runs in to a second issue:

    $ python tools/config_builder.py full_genesis > mycustomgenesis.json
    $ sed -e 's/"code": "/"code": "0x/g' mycustomgenesis.json >mycustomgenesis2.json
    $ geth --datadir /tmp/privategeth init mycustomgenesis2.json
    Fatal: invalid genesis file: hex string has length 2, want 64 for Hash
    $

I'm unsure of exactly what is causing the second issue, but it looks like it is due to the key for the  "storage" entries created by the smart contracts being addresses whereas geth expects them to be hashes, for example

    $ cat mycustomgenesis2.json | jq '.alloc[].storage | select(length > 0)'
    {
      "0x06": "0x48302e3100000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000000008"
    }
    $

Although not a fix, the fact that this is the issue can be shown by removing the storage objects from the genesis JSON:

    $ python tools/config_builder.py full_genesis > mycustomgenesis.json
    $ sed -e 's/"code": "/"code": "0x/g' mycustomgenesis.json >mycustomgenesis2.json
    $ cat mycustomgenesis2.json | jq -c 'del(.alloc[].storage)' >mycustomgenesis3.json
    $ geth --datadir /tmp/privategeth init mycustomgenesis3.json
    Fatal: invalid genesis file: missing 0x prefix for hex data
    $

The third issue is that the 'extraData' field in mk_genesis of tools/genesis_builder.py isn't hex-encoded.  The line

    genesis['extraData'] = CLUSTER_NAME

should be

    genesis['extraData'] = '0x' + CLUSTER_NAME.encode('hex')

At which point the chain can be created:

    $ python tools/config_builder.py full_genesis > mycustomgenesis.json
    $ sed -e 's/"code": "/"code": "0x/g' mycustomgenesis.json >mycustomgenesis2.json
    $ cat mycustomgenesis2.json | jq -c 'del(.alloc[].storage)' >mycustomgenesis3.json
    $ geth --datadir /tmp/privategeth init mycustomgenesis3.json
    WARN [05-25|12:36:10] No etherbase set and no accounts found as default 
    INFO [05-25|12:36:10] Allocated cache and file handles         database=/tmp/privategeth/geth/chaindata cache=16 handles=16
    INFO [05-25|12:36:10] Successfully wrote genesis state         database=chaindata                       hash=a523ae…454e0c
    INFO [05-25|12:36:10] Allocated cache and file handles         database=/tmp/privategeth/geth/lightchaindata cache=16 handles=16
    INFO [05-25|12:36:10] Successfully wrote genesis state         database=lightchaindata                       hash=a523ae…454e0c
    $

## Tasklist

- [ ] Ensure that "code" values are written as correct hex starting with '0x'
- [ ] Ensure that storage keys and values are written in a way suitable for geth to import
- [ ] Merge pending PR to fix creation of 'extraData' field when creating the genesis json

### System Description

Raiden from master
geth 1.6.1-stable-021c3c28 running on linux (also tested with master 1.6.2-unstable-b596b4ba)
All Python requirements taken from requirements.txt / requirements-dev.txt

issue_title: The spawn/fork issue.
issue_body:
We have had a few mysterious resource sharing issue that was solved by [switching from fork to spawn process starting mechanism](https://github.com/vatlab/SOS/blob/62ee6ec9c06ed50fac6e0115eb3d5ff357262620/sos/__main__.py#L251). This also helps

1. Debug the code because some resources/settings (e.g. verbosity) was shared and not correctly passed.
2. Windows compatibility because windows does not support spawn.

However, it is proven that the spawn method is a lot slower than fork. Although this 1 to 2 second difference is not noticeable for real-world applications, it is very obvious for testing (of small scripts) and for first-expressions of users. It should be better to switch back to fork under mac/linux at some point.

The suspect of the problem was the singleton `env` class because we are copying/sharing a singleton class across processes. Perhaps we can get rid of this global singleton class.

This has to be tagged as later because correctness if more important than performance now.

issue_title: Killing workers with running tasks
issue_body:
We want the ability to kill workers with running tasks. Some things to think through and figure out beforehand:

1. When workers notify the scheduler that the task has been completed, the scheduler should not mark the worker as available.

2. Think through what to do if the scheduler receives a `SubmitTask` or `PutObj` from a worker that has been marked for dying.

3. The driver or a worker may have called pull on an object that may never be delivered. (Maybe send some kind of Object Unavailable reply to the object stores so that they notify the workers?)

issue_title: Replace UT data structures with C++ STL.
issue_body:
We began switching from C to C++ in #321. Everything now compiles with a C++ compiler, but the code is still written in the style of a C project. Instead of converting everything all at once to the style of a C++ project, things will happen more gradually.

One immediate benefit is that we can now use the C++ standard library. We've been using some macro-based data structures from here https://troydhanson.github.io/uthash/, which have worked quite well, but are much less convenient than the C++ standard library. The conversion from UT_hash and UT_array to the C++ STL is already happening in various places including #324 and #392. However, there are a bunch more places where it needs to happen.

issue_title: Support job submission
issue_body:
Currently ray requires users to log into the cluster to submit their code, it would be more convenient to allow them to manage their jobs via a web portal. 
And sometimes remote execution for actors/tasks defined in separate files would not work because the dependencies are not carried over. A general way to support this is to deploy the user code and dependencies to all the nodes in the cluster. There's a previous discussion on this [here](https://github.com/ray-project/ray/issues/2327).

We propose to provide the functionality to allow user to submit, cancel, and get status for a job from a web portal, and also automatically deploy code and dependencies for a job at submission time.  To implement we plan to add a http server which would run inside ray cluster, and an agent which runs on each node.  More details are listed in this [proposal](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1LUc8ZRpm2aL6rSg5h91N7_OjodeOtEVmC1NydHMncIc/edit).

issue_title: [V3 Permissions] 3rd party checks required modifications.
issue_body:
With the current model for hooking third party checks into the permissions cog, we need permissions to be loaded first and not be reloaded. This was a design oversight on my part. 

I'll be postponing my other WIP stuff to resolve this in a satisfactory manner prior to b16, but this will require changes to how other checks which want to integrate into the permissions cog rather than operate separately need to function.

For anyone currently designing a 3rd party cog which uses these hooks: 
After the intended changes, any function which is an attribute of your cog should be possible to use as an integrated check. Each cog will get 1 before and 1 after check with expected names.

`__red_permissions_before` and `__red_permissions_after`

issue_title: No way to create `MetadataType` without database connection
issue_body:
In order to construct `MetadataType` object from yaml definition one has to have access to connected db index. No information is queried from the DB during construction. It's just that `doc->MetadataType` conversion is part of the index api attached to an `index` object

```python
m = dc.index.metadata_types.from_doc(doc)
```

`MetadataType` class is just a container of "dataset search fields", the type of the search fields will be different for different index implementations, but there is nothing that depends on the state stored inside the db. 

I propose that 

- Database index driver should expose constructor method for `MetadataType`
- We should have "dataset search fields" implementation that is not tied to any DB driver

If I have "metadata definition yaml" and "dataset yaml" I should be able to parse "dataset yaml" according to "metadata definition yaml" without connecting to the database, and even without deciding what db index should be used.

I believe this limitation is impacting testing in a negative way, a lot of the tests that could have been "unit tests" are now forced into "integration test" category, and integration tests are harder to write and are slower to run.

issue_title: getWaitDuration is defined in all batch systems but remains unused
issue_body:
Method [`getWaitDuration()`](https://github.com/DataBiosphere/toil/blob/master/src/toil/batchSystems/abstractGridEngineBatchSystem.py#L394) is defined in all batch systems but is never called in the codebase. The difference between [`getWaitDuration()`](https://github.com/DataBiosphere/toil/blob/master/src/toil/batchSystems/abstractGridEngineBatchSystem.py#L394) and [`sleepSeconds`](https://github.com/DataBiosphere/toil/blob/master/src/toil/batchSystems/abstractGridEngineBatchSystem.py#L397) is yet not clear to me. However, I think if we don't want to overload batch systems, `statePollingWait` should somehow honor `getWaitDuration()`  [here](https://github.com/DataBiosphere/toil/blob/master/src/toil/batchSystems/abstractGridEngineBatchSystem.py#L184).  For example, only use `statePollingWait` if greater than `getWaitDuration()`:

```python
        def checkOnJobs(self):
            ....
            polling_wait = self.getWaitDuration()
            current_wait = (datetime.now() - self._checkOnJobsTimestamp).total_seconds()

            if self.boss.config.statePollingWait > polling_wait:
                 polling_wait = self.boss.config.statePollingWait

            if self._checkOnJobsTimestamp and current_wait < polling_wait:
                return self._checkOnJobsCache
           .... 
```

issue_title: Headers parsing during Request creation
issue_body:
Hi,

I am not sure that what I'm seeing is a bug or the intended behaviour. In `common.py`, `class Request`, line 380:
`self.headers = CaseInsensitiveDict(encode(headers or {}))`
The headers are parsed into a `CaseInsensitiveDict`, which means `self.headers['content-type']`, `self.headers['Content-Type']` and `self.headers['cONteNt-tYpE']` will all return the same value BUT the keys of the dictionary are still the ones with which the items were defined.

Line 386, all the `_params` are set to None, with full lower case keys. 

Then line 406 
`self._params.update(self.headers)`
So if some `_params` were set in the headers they should update the values here. Problem is, the headers all have first-letter capitalization. So passing a token through header will lead to have 

```
self._params
    {"token": None, "Token": token_value}

request.token
    None
```

Is this the intended behaviour ? I guess not.

issue_title: Next release will be 1.0
issue_body:
My plan is for the next release of Tockloader to be 1.0. The core interface hasn't changed in months, and only the `--jtag` (and related) flags have changed (although the old ones are still supported). No one seems to be complaining about the interface, and I've been happy with it.

I think that commands and features will continue to be added (and have been), but the general install/uninstall/flash/listen interface will be formally stabilized.

issue_title: get_codec modifies its argument
issue_body:
The ``get_codec`` function modifies its argument, popping the "id" key. This can lead to surprising behaviour such as:

```python
config = {"id": "json"}
codec = get_codec(config) # Works
codec = get_codec(config) # fails.
```

I suggest adding ``config = dict(config) `` at the start of the function. The performance implications should be minimal I think.

issue_title: image publish throws exception if init has not been called
newline should be replaced with " " (not "") in tweet text
issue_body:
in tweet.py getText() newlines are replaced with "", this leads to problems with extracting hashtags, for instance:
```
@fred
#myhash
somethingelse
```

is collapsed to
```
@fred#myhashsomethingelse
```
and `myhashsomethingelse` is extracted as the hashtag

issue_title: `strict=True` and its `KeyError`
issue_body:
I think `strict=True` is a poor choice of name in the context of `create` and `move`.  I initially thought it was to check security.  Anyway I have the feeling it may mean a number of things to different people.

Also, `KeyError` is a poor choice to denote that an ID was already taken.  The stack behind a call to `api.create` or `api.move` is huge, and `KeyError` very common.  Thus, you'll end up with `KeyError`s that do not mean "ID already taken".

issue_title: Standard logging module failed when importing zmq
issue_body:
Hi,

I Use pyzmq 2.1.11 and Python 2.6.6 on XP.
It seems as if that the import of zmq disable the logging module.

With the code: 
''
import logging
if __name__ == "__main__":
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
    logging.info('main')
''

I got: 
INFO:root:main

With the code: 
''
import logging
import zmq

if __name__ == "__main__":
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
    logging.info('main')
    print zmq.__version__
''    

I got: 
2.1.11

what's wrong ?


Regards,
Christophe

issue_title: hybrid_properties
issue_body:
I was under the impression [issue 11](https://github.com/FormAlchemy/formalchemy/pull/11) would include hybrid_properties but I cannot make them work. Consider the following example where *gender* is a synonym for *_gender*:

      from formalchemy import Column
      from formalchemy.forms import FieldSet
      from sqlalchemy import Table, Unicode, Integer, create_engine, MetaData
      from sqlalchemy.ext.declarative import declarative_base
      from sqlalchemy.ext.hybrid import hybrid_property
      from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker
      session = sessionmaker()
      Session = session()
      engine = create_engine('postgresql://usr:pass@localhost/db', echo=True)
      Session.configure(bind=engine)
      m = MetaData()
      m.bind = engine
      base = declarative_base(metadata=m)
                                                                                   
      class Test(base):
          __tablename__ = "example"
          id = Column(Integer, autoincrement=True, primary_key=True)
          name = Column(Unicode(100), nullable=False)
          _gender = Column(Unicode(1), nullable=False, default=True)
         @hybrid_property
         def gender(self):
              return self._gender
         @gender.setter
         def gender(self, value):
              if value.upper() not in ('F', 'M'):
                  raise(Exception(u'Gender must be F or M'))
              else:
                  self._gender = value.upper()
                                                                                   
      m.create_all()
                                                                                   
      fs1 = FieldSet(Test())
      fs1.configure(include=[fs1.name, fs1.gender])
      fs1.render()

When I execute it, I'm getting:

      Traceback (most recent call last):
        File "<ipython-input-3-37113d7b6e1e>", line 1, in <module>
          import property_fa as f
        File "property_fa.py", line 35, in <module>
          fs1.configure(include=[fs1.name, fs1.gender])
        File "/home/mariano/Code/example/env/lib/python2.7/site-packages/FormAlchemy-1.4.1-py2.7.egg/formalchemy/forms.py", line 773, in __getattr__
          raise AttributeError(attrname)
      AttributeError: gender

If I use *_gender* as the included field, of course it works however it bypasses my property control.

TIA,
Mariano
